definitions: To look directly and unflinchingly at someone, often to convey sincerity, challenge, or defiance.
To make direct eye contact with someone, especially when speaking truthfully or confronting them.
examples: If you're telling the truth, look me dead in the eye.
He couldn't look me dead in the eye when he admitted his mistake.
She dared him to look her dead in the eye and repeat the lie.
lemma: look someone dead in the eye
source: look me dead in the eye
